carbon_policy_signal.item: EU ETS review launched by Commission in July; Poland vocal opposition signals member-state resistance to carbon price floor or tighter supply cap.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Regulatory uncertainty on MSR, supply cap, or sectoral tightening creates forward EUA curve volatility; carbon at 39th pctile exposed to bullish repricing if floor is tabled.

risk_flags: Storage at 40.48% (17th pctile, 13.9pp below seasonal) — June-July refill pace critical to summer-autumn power stack.
EU-US trade accord vote June 16 may reprrice LNG tariffs, shifting TTF-HH arb and power-gas fuel-switch economics.
ETS review flagged July amid Poland pushback — regulatory uncertainty on carbon floor/cap could trigger EUA volatility.
GB Power at 124.85 EUR/MWh (90th pctile) with Clean Spark at 41.24 suggests GB-EU interconnect stress or local supply tightness.

base.summary: Storage deficit sustains thermal premium; Clean Spark holds 90th+ pctile absent renewable rebound or demand softening.

downside.summary: Renewable share rebounds sharply (wind/solar forecast >60th pctile); storage injection accelerates above seasonal; trade accord delays or softens tariff terms.

upside.summary: EU-US trade accord vote (June 16) narrows LNG tariffs; shadow-fleet sanctions persist, lifting crude-to-power arb; storage refill pace weak — thermal dispatch stays elevated.

themes: Thermal merit-order dominance: Clean Spark extended, storage tight
GB power elevated (90th pctile); EU storage structural deficit risks H2 refill
Geopolitical: Trade accord, shadow-fleet sanctions, ETS review July
Renewables suppressed (35th pctile, -16.17% daily) — thermal call stays elevated
top_drivers: Clean Spark at 93rd pctile (41.24 EUR/MWh) driven by storage deficit (13.9pp below seasonal) and low renewables (35th pctile, -16.17% daily) — sustained thermal call.
GB Power at 90th pctile (124.85 EUR/MWh) suggests GB-EU interconnect stress or local constraint; DE Power at 80th pctile (151.73 EUR/MWh) tracks continental merit-order pressure.

Geopolitical tail risks: EU-US trade accord (June 16) repricing LNG tariffs; shadow-fleet sanctions supporting crude, lifting peaking-plant economics; ETS review (July) creating regulatory overhang on carbon.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 93rd percentile (41.24 EUR/MWh) amid 13.9pp storage deficit signals sustained thermal-dispatch premium; EU-US trade accord vote June 16 adds LNG cost repricing risk.

watchlist.why: At 13.9pp below seasonal (40.48% full), June-July injection pace will set H2 power risk and gas shoulder-season pricing.
Tariff landscape on LNG imports directly affects TTF-HH spread and EU power-gas fuel-switch marginal cost.
Commission review amid Poland's opposition creates forward-curve uncertainty on carbon price floor or cap tightening; EUA currently 39th pctile.
At 35th pctile (-16.17% daily drop), any multi-day wind drought extends thermal call; Clean Spark at 93rd pctile sensitive to further renewable weakness.
Reduced Russian crude via EU-linked insurers supports Brent; higher crude-to-power arb in peaking generation.

watchlist_dated.name: EU ETS review (July) signal watch
watchlist_dated.why: Commission regulatory direction on carbon floor/cap tightening affects forward EUA curve and power dispatch incentives.

watchlist_dated.name: EU-US trade accord final vote
watchlist_dated.why: June 16 plenary vote on tariff terms reshapes LNG import cost, TTF-HH spread, and power-gas fuel-switch slope.
